title: Flame Grilled Smoky Uruguayan Beet Skillet
description: This sweet and tender Uruguayan dish features beets, smoke, and is pescatarian friendly, perfect for a midnight craving. It is cooked in an iron teppan skillet.
instructions: Preheat the iron teppan skillet over medium high heat for 5 minutes.
Add olive oil to the preheated skillet and swirl it around to coat the bottom.
Add diced red onion to the skillet and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add diced beets to the skillet and cook for 10 minutes, until they are tender and lightly charred.
Add smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
Add shrimp to the skillet and cook until they are pink and fully cooked, about 5 minutes.
Squeeze lemon juice over the top of the skillet and stir to combine.
Remove the skillet from the heat and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
